triage.impactDetail: Node R02-M1-N0 showing intermittent parity errors. Running workload migrated to healthy nodes.
triage.compoundScenario: Scenario 7: Hardware Degradation (repeat ECC-corrected parity ⇒ module cell wear ⇒ quarantine before uncorrectable fault)

metadata.logTemplate: R{*}-M{*}-N{*}-C:J{*}-U{*} RAS KERNEL INFO instruction cache parity error corrected
metadata.rawLogSample: - 1117838570 2005.06.03 R02-M1-N0-C:J12-U11 2005-06-03-15.42.50.675872 R02-M1-N0-C:J12-U11 RAS KERNEL INFO instruction cache parity error corrected

hypotheses.title: Memory module degradation on node R02-M1-N0
hypotheses.reasoning: Repeated parity errors localized to the same physical address range suggest cell-level degradation rather than random transient upset.

hypotheses.title: Cosmic ray induced transient bit flip
hypotheses.reasoning: Single-event upsets are common at supercomputer scale and can produce isolated parity errors without any underlying hardware defect.

hypotheses.title: Thermal stress from adjacent high-utilization node
hypotheses.reasoning: Sustained high utilization on the neighbor node could raise local ambient temperature enough to destabilize nearby memory cells.

evidence.text: Instruction cache parity error corrected on R02-M1-N0 (ECC recovered)
evidence.sourceLogSnippet: - 1117838570 2005.06.03 R02-M1-N0-C:J12-U11 2005-06-03-15.42.50.675872 R02-M1-N0-C:J12-U11 RAS KERNEL INFO instruction cache parity error corrected

evidence.text: Third parity error recorded from the same node within a 7-day window
evidence.sourceLogSnippet: 2026-04-18 10:18:50 INFO ras.aggregator: R02-M1-N0 parity error (3rd occurrence in 7d window)

evidence.text: Adjacent node R02-M1-N1 sustained 85% utilization over preceding 6 hours
evidence.sourceLogSnippet: 2026-04-18 04:20:00 INFO scheduler: node R02-M1-N1 util avg=0.85 window=6h

actionPlan.rationale: Prevents additional workloads from landing on a node that is already showing repeat parity errors.

actionPlan.action: Run ECC memory diagnostic against suspected module J12-U11
actionPlan.rationale: Confirms whether errors localize to a specific memory module versus being distributed across the node.

actionPlan.action: Migrate any pending workloads to healthy nodes in the same rack
Schedule hardware replacement window for memory module J12-U11
actionPlan.rationale: Module replacement is the definitive fix for cell-level memory degradation; quarantine is only a temporary containment.

executiveSummary.headline: Repeat parity errors on R02-M1-N0 point to memory module degradation; workloads migrated, no service impact
executiveSummary.impact: No customer-facing impact. Single compute node quarantined from the scheduler. Running workloads migrated automatically to healthy nodes in the same rack.
executiveSummary.suspectedCause: Single-bit parity error was corrected by ECC, but this is the third occurrence from the same node within a seven-day window, indicating a deteriorating memory module rather than a transient cosmic-ray bit flip.
executiveSummary.mitigations: Node quarantined from the scheduler. ECC diagnostics queued against the suspected module to localize the fault before replacement.
executiveSummary.prevention: Tighten per-node parity-error frequency thresholds to quarantine earlier, schedule preventive module health checks on nodes nearing replacement age, and note that rollback is not applicable for hardware-level incidents.
